PowerShell only for Windows-specific leaf tasks. |\n| **Python** | Allowed for specialist tools, not the controller |\n\n### Shell Policy\n\n- **Bash** is the default shell for ADF operations, scripts, and CLI entry point\n- **PowerShell** is restricted to Windows-specific tasks where Bash cannot reach (e.g., Windows registry, COM objects, native Windows APIs)\n- ADF is OS-agnostic ג€” Bash provides the cross-platform baseline\n\n### LLM Model Configuration\n\nADF uses CLI-based LLM calls via `node:child_process`. No API SDKs. Codex is the primary provider (more tokens available), Claude is the fallback.\n\n| Role | Primary (Codex) | Reasoning | Fallback (Claude) | Effort |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| **Classifier** | `gpt-5.3-codex-spark` | `medium` | `claude --model haiku` | `medium` |\n| **COO** | `gpt-5.4` | `xhigh` | `claude --model opus` | `max` |\n\n**Classifier**: intent classification only. Fast, cheap model. No action permissions (`--sandbox read-only`). No bypass.\n\n**COO**: executive reasoning, planning, memory operations. Strongest available model. Full permissions:\n- Codex: `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox`\n- Claude: `--dangerously-skip-permissions`\n\n### LLM Call Pattern\n\n```\nUser input ג†’ Controller\n  ג†’ Classifier call (Codex spark, fast)\n  ג†’ Route to workflow\n  ג†’ COO call (Codex gpt-5.4 or Claude opus, strong)\n  ג†’ Validate + commit state\n```\n\nBoth calls use `codex exec` / `claude --print` for non-interactive execution. Prompt passed as argument, response captured from stdout/file.\n\n---\n\n## Architecture Model\n\n### Core Principle\n\nAgent as a **stateless reducer** (12-factor-agents). The controller is a deterministic orchestrator ג€” not an LLM, not a drifting session. Each turn is a pure function: `next_event = agent(thread)`.\n\n### Turn Flow\n\n```\nUser input\n  ג†“\nController ingress\n  ג†“\nLoad current state\n  - COO role\n  - current contracts\n  - active summary\n  - open loops\n  - tool registry\n  - rule versions\n  ג†“\nIntent classification\n  - bounded classifier model call\n  ג†“\nSelect workflow\n  - direct COO response\n  - tool path\n  - specialist path\n  - clarification / pushback\n  ג†“\nRetrieve scoped context\n  - only what this turn needs\n  ג†“\nExecute selected path\n  - COO call and/or tool/specialist call\n  ג†“\nValidate outputs\n  - contracts\n  - required artifacts\n  - status correctness\n  - approval boundaries\n  ג†“\nCommit state\n  - summary\n  - open loops\n  - minutes/context\n  - audit/logs\n  - resume/session state\n  ג†“\nReturn response\n```\n\n### Key Components\n\n1. **Thread model** ג€” typed event list (Zod). Event types: `user_input`, `classifier_result`, `tool_call`, `tool_result`, `coo_response`, `error`, `human_request`, `state_commit`. The thread IS the state ג€” single source of truth per session.\n\n2. **Controller loop** ג€” deterministic `thread ג†’ next_event` reducer. TypeScript program, not a framework invocation. Every routing decision, gate, and error path is explicit code.\n\n3. **Context engineer** ג€” `thread_to_prompt()` function. Assembles thread events + PARA facts + auto-recalled memory + daily residue + owned prompts into optimized context per LLM call.\n\n4. **Intent classifier** ג€” small bounded LLM call per turn. Zod-validated structured output. Decides: direct COO response, tool path, specialist path, clarification, pushback.\n\n5. **COO agent** ג€” user-facing reasoning worker. Called as a function per turn with assembled context. Emits structured outputs. Does not maintain internal state.\n\n6. **Tool schema registry** ג€” tools defined as Zod types. Discovery via registry, not hardcoded lists. Each tool has a contract: input schema, output schema, side effects, approval requirements.\n\n### Operational Principles\n\n- Input first goes to the controller, never directly to the COO\n- Intent classification is model-assisted (script alone is not enough for natural language)\n- Controller remains deterministic even when invoking a classifier\n- Fresh model calls per turn (classifier, COO, specialists)\n- Continuity comes from externalized state (contracts, summaries, open loops, PARA), not from trusting a raw running session\n\n### Component Contract Rule\n\nEvery component must have: root folder, contract, source files, build artifacts, runtime artifacts, test surface, and audit surface.\n\n### Agent Role Governance Rule\n\n**Every LLM-powered agent must have a defined role and strict contract created through the agent-role-builder tool.** No exceptions ג€” this applies to all models regardless of capability level (including cheap/fast classifier models). The review board exists because:\n\n1. A single LLM never thinks about everything\n2. Different LLMs think differently and catch blind spots of other LLMs\n3. There are only a handful of core agents ג€” the high effort is justified to prevent drift\n\nRoles are created via governed multi-LLM review (Codex + Claude pairs), validated against schemas, and frozen only when no material pushback remains.\n\nSee [components-and-layers.md](components-and-layers.md) for the full component/layer taxonomy.\n\n### No Shortcuts Rule\n\n**No component is done until it has been through its own governance process and the evidence exists.** This means:\n\n- No manual seeds, no happy path assumptions, no deferred fixes\n- Every tool must eat its own dog food\n- Evidence-based development: if there is no artifact proving it works, it does not work\n- This applies to every tool, role, and component without exception\n- If something cannot be fully completed now, it probably never will be ג€” do it right the first time\n\n### Tool Integration Rule\n\n**llm-tool-builder must always call agent-role-builder** when creating a new tool. Verify: both tools produced valid governed artifacts ג€” evidence exists\n\n### Review Process Rule\n\n**Every component that goes through governed review must have:**\n- A `rulebook.json` in its directory ג€” accumulated high-level rules from past failures\n- A `review-prompt.json` in its directory ג€” defines domain-specific review focus\n- If the component has multiple LLM agents, each gets its own subfolder with its own rulebook and review prompt\n\n**Review rounds follow a structured protocol:**\n- Implementer produces delta compliance map (full sweep on first and last round only)\n- Implementer produces fix items map with accept/reject freedom (rounds 2+)\n- Reviewer produces structured verdict (approved/conditional/reject) with conceptual groups, severity, redesign guidance\n- Reviewer responds to implementer rejections (accept_fix/reject_fix/accept_rejection/reject_rejection)\n- Learning engine extracts rules between review and fix ג€” updates the component's rulebook.json\n\n**Arbitration is minor-only.** No arbitration on blocking/major items. Result is `frozen_with_conditions`, invoker decides acceptance.\n\n**Budget exhaustion:** each reviewer picks 1 most important fix, implementer fixes those only, run exits as `resume_required`. No silent swallowing.\n\nSee [review-process-architecture.md](review-process-architecture.md) for full specification.\n\n### Learning Engine Rule\n\n**The learning engine (`shared/learning-engine/`) runs between every review and fix cycle.** It extracts generalizable rules from review feedback and updates the specific component's `rulebook.json`. Same generic engine, different domain prompt per component (loaded from `review-prompt.json`).\n\n### Three-Tier Post-Mortem Rule\n\nEvery governed component has three levels of post-mortem:\n- **Run post-mortem** (after every round): KPI snapshot + rulebook update. No self-healing.\n- **Cycle post-mortem** (on terminal verdict): job summary + deeper learning + self-healing. Lightweight if frozen in budget.\n- **Jobs post-mortem** (every N cycles, background): component-wide KPIs + systemic improvement. Deletes raw data, keeps commit IDs for audit.\n\n### Error Escalation Rule\n\n**When any governed component hits an unrecoverable error, it must stop immediately and produce a structured bug report.** No silent retries, no parse hacks. The governance layer receives the bug report and attempts auto-fix via builder (llm-tool-builder when complete, Codex agent interim). If fix succeeds, relaunch from the failed step. If fix fails or error persists, full stop with error chain documented.\n\nBug report must include: what failed, where (step/phase), execution context, input that caused failure (file path or verbatim JSON), expected format, component source_path, and provenance.\n\nSee [review-process-architecture.md](review-process-architecture.md) for full specification.\n\n### Telemetry Rule\n\n**Every operation that consumes resources must emit telemetry.** This includes:\n- LLM calls (tokens in/out, cost estimate, latency, model, primary vs fallback)\n- Memory engine operations (query latency, embedding time, op counts)\n- Tool executions (run time, board rounds, review budget consumed)\n- Turn lifecycle (total time, per-phase breakdown)\n\nTelemetry is:\n- **Async and atomic** ג€” zero latency to caller, fire-and-forget via MCP\n- **Stored in PostgreSQL** ג€” same DB as the memory engine, dedicated `telemetry` table\n- **Queryable by the COO** ג€” via `query_metrics` and `get_cost_summary` MCP tools\n- **Used for governance** ג€” COO warns CEO about costly operations, identifies bottlenecks, drives self-improvement\n\n### Governance Source of Truth\n\nThe **memory engine is the single source of truth** for all governance once it's live:\n\n| What | Storage | Trust Level |\n|---|---|---|\n| Decisions | `content_type: \"decision\"` | locked (immutable) |\n| Rules | `content_type: \"rule\"` | locked (immutable) |\n| Conventions | `content_type: \"convention\"` | reviewed or locked |\n| Settings/thresholds | `content_type: \"setting\"` | reviewed |\n| Telemetry | `telemetry` table | append-only |\n\nDocs in `docs/v0/` are scaffolding ג€” they get imported as locked database records. Rules are not markdown files ג€” they're database records with trust levels and enforcement.\n\n---\n\n## 6-Layer Memory Stack\n\n| Layer | Name | Purpose |\n|---|---|---|\n| **A** | Routing | MEMORY.md + AGENTS.md ג€” tiny bootstrap/index, not a fact warehouse |\n| **B** | Session Recovery | LCM (lossless-claw) ג€” scoped to COO conversation layer, not controller execution state |\n| **C** | Durable Knowledge | PARA (`projects/`, `areas/`, `resources/`, `archives/`) ג€” single source of truth for facts |\n| **D** | Daily Residue | `memory/YYYY-MM-DD.md` ג€” what happened today, short-horizon continuity |\n| **E** | Auto-Recall | Gigabrain ג€” pre-prompt recall/capture, feeds context engineer |\n| **F** | Cross-Session Graph | OpenStinger (optional) ג€” semantic + temporal recall across sessions |\n\n### Memory Design Principles\n\n1. File truth beats magic ג€” durable truth lives in files, not hidden config\n2. Tiny bootstrap ג€” MEMORY.md and AGENTS.md stay lean and focused\n3. Non-destructive ג€” backups before any modification\n7. The system is built from governed components ג€” each component that uses LLMs must have a defined role created through a multi-LLM review board.\n\nDuring the first attempt to create the agent-role-builder's own role (the bootstrap proof that the tool works), we discovered that the review process itself had fundamental gaps. Across 6 runs and 20+ review rounds, the board couldn't converge ג€” reviewers kept finding new issues at the same rate old ones were fixed.\n\nRoot cause analysis revealed five problems:\n1. Reviewer feedback was flat text lists with no severity or structure ג€” the implementer couldn't tell what was critical vs cosmetic\n2. The implementer received raw JSON blobs instead of actionable fix instructions ג€” it had to re-parse and interpret feedback each round\n3. No institutional memory existed ג€” the same types of issues kept appearing because there was no accumulation of \"don't do this\" rules\n4. Both reviewers ran every round even when one already approved ג€” wasting time and creating noise\n5. No defined process for what happens when reviewers disagree or when the review budget runs out\n\nThis architecture solves all five problems and creates a self-improving system that gets better with every review cycle it runs.\n\n---\n\n## Core Principle: Everything Is Boxed\n\nEvery component in ADF is self-contained in its own directory. All files related to that component live inside its directory ג€” not scattered across the project.\n\nA component may contain multiple LLM agents. Each LLM agent gets its own subfolder with its own rulebook and review prompt. For example, the COO component has controller/, classifier/, intelligence/, and context-engineer/ ג€” each LLM-powered layer (classifier, intelligence) has its own subfolder with its own governance artifacts.\n\nThis means any agent can understand a component by reading its directory. No hunting across the project for related files.\n\nPer-component (or per-LLM-agent subfolder):\n- **Source code** ג€” the implementation\n- **Rulebook** (`rulebook.json`) ג€” accumulated rules from past review failures\n- **Review prompt** (`review-prompt.json`) ג€” defines what reviewers should focus on for this component\n- **Run artifacts** ג€” permanent evidence of every review cycle\n- **Role definition** ג€” if the component uses LLMs, its governed role lives here too\n\n---\n\n## The Rulebook\n\nEvery component that goes through governed review must have a `rulebook.json` in its own directory. This is the component's institutional memory.\n\n**What rules are:**\nRules are high-level, generalized core essence directives distilled from real review failures. They capture the principle behind the failure, not the specific incident. Examples:\n- \"Define authority precedence explicitly when multiple governance layers exist\"\n- \"Never reference the same concept with different phrasings across sections without a canonical definition\"\n- \"Completion criteria must be testable against every terminal state, not just the happy path\"\n- \"Always do null checks on scope resolution results\"\n- \"Don't swallow errors ג€” surface them with source and context\"\n\n**What rules are NOT:**\n- Not copies of reviewer feedback (\"round 2 said authority chain is ambiguous\")\n- Not implementation details (\"add a null check on line 47\")\n- Not temporary fixes (\"workaround for the encoding bug\")\n\n**Rule structure (JSON):**\nEach rule has an ID, the generalized principle, the source review cycle that produced it, and a version. Rules can be superseded but never silently deleted ג€” history is preserved in git.\n\n**Who creates rules:**\nThe learning engine (described below) extracts rules from review feedback. The implementer doesn't write rules ג€” they follow them.\n\n---\n\n## The Learning Engine\n\nA shared generic service that lives in `shared/learning-engine/`. Its job is to analyze review feedback and extract generalizable rules that prevent the same failures from recurring.\n\n**How it works:**\nAfter each review round and before any fix attempt, the learning engine receives the reviewer feedback and asks: \"What generalizable principle does this failure reveal?\" It produces new rulebook entries (or identifies that existing rules already cover the issue). It updates the specific component's `rulebook.json` ג€” the file in that component's directory, not a generic global file.\n\n**Why it's generic:**\nThe extraction logic is the same regardless of what's being reviewed. \"What pattern does this failure reveal?\" works for role definitions, TypeScript code, system prompts, and architecture documents. The only thing that changes is the prompt context.\n\n**How prompts are used:**\nEach component holds a `review-prompt.json` in its own directory. This prompt tells the learning engine what domain it's operating in (design review, code review, prompt review, architecture review) and what kinds of rules are relevant. The learning engine loads this prompt to contextualize its analysis. Without it, the engine doesn't know what domain patterns to look for.\n\n**Why it runs between review and fix:**\nIf the implementer fixes issues without first extracting the rule, the same class of issue will appear in future runs. The learning step ensures the rulebook grows before the fix is attempted. The implementer then reads the updated rulebook before fixing, which means the fix addresses the root pattern, not just the specific symptom.\n\n---\n\n## The Review Process (Per Round)\n\nEach review round follows this sequence:\n\n### Step 1: Implementer produces or revises artifacts\nThe implementer (an LLM) creates or revises the component artifact (role definition, code, prompt, etc.). On the first round, this is generated from the request. On subsequent rounds, it's revised based on prior feedback.\n\n### Step 2: Implementer produces delta compliance map\nThe implementer checks its work against the rulebook and produces a JSON compliance map showing:\n- Which rules were checked\n- Where in the artifact each rule is satisfied\n- Only for sections that changed (delta) ג€” not the entire artifact on middle rounds\n- References the git commit or artifact version being checked\n\nThis gives the reviewer a head start ג€” they can verify the implementer's claims instead of rediscovering known issues from scratch.\n\n**Full sweep rule:**\n- First round: full compliance map (all rules against entire artifact)\n- Middle rounds: delta compliance map (only changed sections)\n- Final round (when reviewer finds no pushbacks): full sweep to catch cross-cutting issues\n- This balances thoroughness with efficiency\n\n### Step 3: Implementer produces fix items map (rounds 2+)\nOn subsequent rounds (after a review rejection), the implementer produces a fix items map showing for each reviewer finding:\n- `accepted`: what was changed, where, and a summary of the fix\n- `rejected`: why the finding is invalid (missing context, contradicts another requirement, etc.)\n\nThis serves as both proof of fix AND reviewer context for the next round. The reviewer doesn't have to guess what changed ג€” the map tells them.\n\n### Step 4: Review\nReviewers (Codex + Claude pair minimum) independently review the artifact. Their feedback uses a structured contract (JSON):\n- **Verdict**: `approved` (ready to freeze), `conditional` (minor fixes only), or `reject` (blocking/major issues)\n- **Conceptual groups**: findings grouped by root cause, not flat lists. Each group has:\n  - Severity: `blocking`, `major`, `minor`, or `suggestion`\n  - Specific findings with source section references\n  - Redesign guidance: what to do about it\n- **Residual risks**: things that aren't blocking but need tracking\n- **Strengths**: what's good (not just criticism)\n- **Fix items decision map** (rounds 2+): for each item in the implementer's fix map:\n  - `accept_fix`: the fix resolves the finding\n  - `reject_fix`: the fix is insufficient (with reason why)\n  - `accept_rejection`: the implementer was right to reject the finding\n  - `reject_rejection`: the implementer's rejection is invalid (with reason why)\n\nThis creates a proper negotiation protocol between implementer and reviewer, not a one-way broadcast. The implementer has freedom to push back on findings that lack context or contradict other requirements, and the reviewer must explicitly respond to each pushback.\n\n### Step 5: On review failure (reject verdict)\nWhen a reviewer rejects:\n1. Learning engine digests the feedback and updates the component's `rulebook.json`\n2. A fresh implementer receives: current artifact + updated rulebook + actionable fix checklist + prior compliance map\n3. The implementer first mechanically walks every rule in the rulebook against the artifact, then addresses the specific feedback items\n4. The implementer produces both a new compliance map (delta) and a fix items map\n5. The round proceeds to the next review cycle\n\n### Step 6: On review pass (approved/conditional)\nWhen all reviewers approve or go conditional:\n- Conditional items are fixed (specific minor changes only)\n- Leader confirms freeze\n- Artifacts promoted to canonical location\n- Decision log and board summary written\n\n---\n\n## Split Verdict Handling\n\nWhen one reviewer approves and another rejects:\n\n1. Fix only the rejecting reviewer's findings\n2. Re-run only the rejecting reviewer in the next round ג€” don't waste the approving reviewer's time\n3. When the rejecting reviewer approves or goes conditional, run one final sanity check with the previously-approving reviewer to catch any regressions the fixes may have introduced\n4. Both must approve/conditional before the artifact can freeze\n\nThis cuts review cycles significantly ג€” prior runs re-ran both reviewers every round even when one was satisfied.\n\n---\n\n## Arbitration (Minor Items Only)\n\nArbitration is strictly limited to prevent swallowing real issues:\n\n**When arbitration is allowed:**\n- Only on `minor` and `suggestion` severity items where reviewers disagree on whether something needs fixing\n- The leader can decide \"this is acceptable as-is\" for these items only\n- The decision is logged with full rationale\n\n**When arbitration is NOT allowed:**\n- Never on `blocking` or `major` severity items\n- Never to override a `reject` verdict\n- Never to force a freeze when material pushback remains\n\n**Budget exhaustion protocol (final round):**\nWhen the review budget is about to run out and blocking/major items remain:\n- The artifact does NOT freeze. Blocking issues cannot be swallowed.\n- Each reviewer picks their single most important remaining fix (ultimatum)\n- The implementer fixes those specific items only\n- The decision is logged with full audit trail: what was fixed, what was deferred, and why\n- The run exits as `resume_required` with deferred items documented\n- The cycle post-mortem (described below) analyzes why the budget wasn't sufficient\n\n**Arbitration result:**\nWhen arbitration is used, the result must reflect it as partial acceptance ג€” `frozen_with_conditions` (distinct from clean `frozen`). The result lists all deferred minor items. The invoking component (the caller that requested the role/review) can then:\n- Accept the conditional result\n- Request more rounds to achieve full acceptance\n\nThe invoker has authority, not the tool.\n\n---\n\n## All Contracts Are JSON\n\nEvery input and output in this architecture is strict JSON with defined schemas. No markdown for machine interfaces. Markdown is for human-readable documentation only.\n\nThis includes:\n- Rulebook entries\n- Compliance maps\n- Fix items maps\n- Review results\n- Learning engine outputs\n- Post-mortem reports\n- Run artifacts\n\nWhy: JSON is parseable, validatable, and unambiguous. Markdown is prose ג€” it can't be mechanically verified. When the compliance map says \"rule R-003 satisfied at <authority> line 12\", that's a verifiable claim. When a markdown file says \"authority is defined\", that's an opinion.\n\n---\n\n## Permanent Audit Trail\n\nAll run artifacts are committed to git. Nothing is volatile. The structure:\n\n```\n<component>/\n  rulebook.json\n  review-prompt.json\n  runs/\n    <job-id>/\n      rounds/\n        round-0/\n          review.json          # reviewer verdicts (structured)\n          learning.json        # learning engine output (new rules)\n          compliance-map.json  # implementer's delta compliance check\n          fix-items-map.json   # implementer's fix/reject map (round 1+)\n          diff-summary.json    # what changed from prior round\n        round-1/\n          ...\n      result.json              # terminal result\n      run-postmortem.json      # tier 1 KPI\n      cycle-postmortem.json    # tier 2 summary (when job completes)\n```\n\n**Commit timing:**\n- **Commit**: at the end of every round (all round artifacts). Preserves evidence atomically per round.\n- **Push**: at the end of every cycle (when job reaches terminal state). Rounds accumulate locally, push happens once when done.\n- Exception: if a round takes unusually long (watchdog timeout), commit before the next round starts so evidence isn't lost if the process crashes.\n\n---\n\n## Error Escalation Pattern (System-Wide)\n\nWhen any governed component hits an unrecoverable error during execution:\n\n### Step 1: Stop Immediately\nDo not retry, do not parse-hack, do not silently continue. The component stops at the point of failure.\n\n### Step 2: Produce Structured Bug Report\nThe bug report (`bug-report.json` in the run directory) must include:\n- **What failed**: error type, error message, stack trace if available\n- **Where**: which step/phase the component was in (e.g., \"Round 2, leader response parsing\")\n- **Context**: current execution state ג€” round number, reviewer verdicts so far, terminal status, what step was being executed\n- **Input that caused the failure**: if it's a file, the full path. If it's JSON input (e.g., raw LLM response), include verbatim or truncated with full version saved to a separate file in the run directory\n- **Expected format**: what the component expected to receive (schema name, example)\n- **Component**: which tool/layer produced the error (source_path)\n- **Provenance**: who was running, which LLM, which model, invocation UUID\n\n### Step 3: Governance Script Receives Bug Report\nThe governance layer (board orchestrator, or COO controller) receives the bug report and decides the next action.\n\n### Step 4: Auto-Fix Attempt\n- Call the builder (llm-tool-builder when complete, Codex agent as interim) with the bug report\n- Builder analyzes the error, proposes a fix, applies it\n- If fix applied: relaunch the component from the step where it stopped (not from scratch)\n\n### Step 5: Verify Fix\n- If relaunched component succeeds: continue execution\n- If same error persists or new error occurs: full stop\n\n### Step 6: Full Stop and Report\n- If auto-fix fails: produce a final error report for the CEO\n- Include: original bug report + fix attempt details + why it failed\n- Run exits as `blocked` with the error chain documented\n\nThis pattern applies to **every governed component** in ADF. Any unrecoverable error follows this escalation path.\n\n---\n\n## Three-Tier Post-Mortem\n\nThe system learns at three different scopes. Each tier has its own trigger, focus, and output. They are NOT nested supersets ג€” each has a distinct scope.\n\n### Tier 1: Run Post-Mortem (run-specific)\n\n- **Scope**: single run (the rounds within one attempt)\n- **Trigger**: after every round completes\n- **Focus**: what happened in this specific run\n- **Does**: captures KPI snapshot (time per round, reviewer verdicts, issue counts, convergence rate) and updates the component's rulebook if the learning engine found new rules\n- **Does NOT**: fix anything or perform self-healing. Run post-mortem is an observer, not an actor. It must be fast and cheap because it runs after every round.\n- **Output**: `run-postmortem.json` in the run directory\n\n### Tier 2: Cycle Post-Mortem (job-specific)\n\n- **Scope**: single job (all runs/attempts for one job)\n- **Trigger**: when the job reaches a terminal verdict (frozen, blocked, resume_required)\n- **Focus**: what happened across all attempts for this specific job\n- **Does**: produces a job summary covering all runs, performs deeper learning analysis (why did it take N runs? what patterns emerged?), and performs self-healing (if the tool itself had a gap, identifies it and feeds a fix request before resuming)\n- **Lightweight case**: if the job froze within budget on the first run, this is just summarizing the single run's findings into a job summary. Very lightweight execution.\n- **Heavy case**: if the job took 4 runs to freeze, this has real work ג€” analyzing WHY it took 4 runs, what broke, what was learned, what was fixed, and whether the fixes actually helped\n- **Output**: `cycle-postmortem.json` ג€” job summary + learning findings + self-improvement recommendations\n\n### Tier 3: Jobs Post-Mortem (component-specific)\n\n- **Scope**: all jobs for a component (across all time)\n- **Trigger**: every N cycles (e.g., 3) or manually triggered. **Runs in a background process** ג€” does not block work.\n- **Focus**: how is the component performing overall\n- **Does**: deepest analysis across all jobs ג€” component-level KPIs (average rounds to freeze, most common failure categories, rule effectiveness scores), generic issue patterns across all jobs, systemic blind spots, and deep self-improvement recommendations that affect the component's architecture or review process itself\n- **Output**: component KPI baseline + generic issue summary + systemic improvement plan\n- **Cleanup**: after analysis, deletes the raw run/round data for analyzed jobs to prevent bloating. The summary must include the deleted jobs' last commit IDs so there is an audit trail back to the git history if deep-dive is ever needed.\n\n**Key distinction:**\n- **Run** captures (specific to rounds within one attempt)\n- **Cycle** fixes (specific to one job across attempts)\n- **Jobs** improves systemically (specific to the component across all jobs)\n\n---\n\n## System-Wide Application\n\nEverything described above applies to every governed component in ADF, not just the agent-role-builder. Any component that uses LLMs and goes through review gets:\n\n- A rulebook in its directory (per LLM agent if multiple agents)\n- A review prompt in its directory (per LLM agent)\n- The learning engine between review and fix\n- Delta compliance maps from the implementer\n- Fix items maps with implementer accept/reject freedom\n- Structured reviewer feedback with fix decision maps\n- Three-tier post-mortem (run/cycle/jobs)\n- Permanent audit trail with defined commit/push points\n- Split-verdict handling\n- Budget exhaustion protocol (no swallowing of blocking issues)\n- Arbitration only on minor items, result reflects partial acceptance\n\nThe shared infrastructure (`shared/learning-engine/`, `shared/llm-invoker/`, `shared/telemetry/`) serves all components equally. The per-component artifacts (rulebook, review prompt, runs) live in each component's directory.\n\n---\n\n## Empirical Evidence: First Review Cycle (Step 2f, 2026-03-28)\n\nThe first application of this review process was a 3-round code review of the ADF shared infrastructure (34 files). Results:\n\n### Timing Baseline\n\n| Round | Duration | Tool Calls | Tokens | Scope |\n|---|---|---|---|---|\n| 1 (cold start) | ~3.5 min | 52 | 95K | Full review of 34 files |\n| 2 (verify fixes) | ~3.5 min | 33 | 50K | Changed files + fix items map |\n| 3 (final sweep) | ~2.5 min | 25 | 64K | Full sweep on clean state |\n| **Total** | **~9.5 min** | **110** | **~210K** | 3 blocking + 5 major resolved |\n\n### Key Finding: Compliance Map Prevents Missed Fixes\n\nRound 2 found a major security issue (R2-1): the Gemini shell injection fix was applied to the canonical invoker but NOT to the copy in shared-imports.ts. A compliance map that listed all locations where each fix applies would have caught this before the reviewer did. This is now system rule SYS-001 in the rule-book-guide.\n\n### Convergence Pattern\n\n- Round 1: 3 blocking, 5 major, 7 minor, 5 suggestions (cold start)\n- Round 2: 9 fixes accepted, 1 rejection accepted, 1 new major (missed copy)\n- Round 3: all fixes verified, 3 rejections accepted, 0 violations on full sweep ג€” **APPROVED**\n\n### Cost Observation\n\nRound 1 (cold start) is the most expensive ג€” reviewer reads everything. Rounds 2-3 are cheaper because the fix items map tells the reviewer exactly what changed. The delta compliance approach is validated: incremental rounds cost ~50-65% of the initial round.\n\n### Evidence\n\n- Round 1: `docs/v0/reviews/step2f-implementation-review.md`\n- Round 2: `docs/v0/reviews/step2f-round2-review.json`\n- Round 3: `docs/v0/reviews/step2f-round3-review.json`\n\r\n\n succeeded in 389ms:\n# Phase 1 Definition Source Pack\n\nStatus: active reference pack\nLast updated: 2026-03-29\nPurpose: preserve the current source set for Phase 1 company-definition, feature-flow, and requirements-gathering decisions so future agents do not need the CEO to restate it.\n\n---\n\n## How To Use This Pack\n\nUse this pack before asking the CEO a new Phase 1 design, workflow, queue, or requirements question.\n\nRequired protocol:\n\n1. Check the relevant sources in this pack first.\n2. Summarize the findings that already exist.\n3. Give a recommendation.\n4. Ask only the smallest unresolved question.\n\nDo not ask the CEO a broad design question if the answer is already present in these sources.\n\nThis pack should also be used for **context recovery** when a fresh agent needs to rebuild the current Phase 1 picture quickly.\n\n---\n\n## Brain Context Recovery Protocol\n\nWhen rebuilding Phase 1 context, do not rely on repo docs alone. Pull the current operating picture from the Brain memory engine as well.\n\n### MCP access shape\n\nThe memory engine is an MCP server over stdio JSON-RPC. The direct call shape is:\n\n1. `initialize`\n2. `notifications/initialized`\n3. `tools/list`\n4. `tools/call`\n\nUse the MCP tool surface before inventing one-off wrappers.\n\n### What to pull from the Brain for context\n\nStart with these brain pulls:\n\n1. `get_context_summary`\n   - pull broad p0/p1 context for the current scope when you need the current executive picture fast\n2. `list_recent`\n   - pull recent changes to see what moved lately\n3. `search_memory`\n   - use for topic-targeted recall such as requirements, decisions, lessons, conventions, context, and prior discussions\n4. `open_loops_manage`\n   - list or search active open items that still matter\n5. `rules_manage`\n   - list or search active governance rules relevant to the current question\n6. `requirements_manage`\n   - list or search stored requirement artifacts when trying to recover current feature truth\n7. `settings_manage`\n   - list or search operating preferences, thresholds, and current company settings\n8. `findings_manage`\n   - search recurring issues or pushed-back findings when they matter to the current lane\n\n### Important note about decisions\n\nThere is no separate decision-list MCP surface today.\n\nTo recover decisions, use:\n\n- `search_memory` with `content_type: \"decision\"` when searching by topic\n- `get_context_summary` and `list_recent` when you need the latest decision-shaped memory in scope\n\n`log_decision` is for writing new decisions, not for broad context retrieval.\n\n### Default context-recovery order\n\nWhen a fresh agent needs to catch up fast:\n\n1. read this source pack\n2. read the current synthesis draft\n3. pull `get_context_summary`\n4. pull `open_loops_manage`\n5. pull `rules_manage`\n6. pull `list_recent`\n7. use `search_memory` for the exact topic being discussed\n\nThis should be the default before asking the CEO to restate old context.\n\n---\n\n## Source Priority\n\nUse the sources in this order:\n\n1. Current synthesis draft\n\n- Source: [phase1-feature-flow-and-executive-briefing-draft.md](C:/ADF/docs/v0/context/phase1-feature-flow-and-executive-briefing-draft.md)\n- Type: current ADF synthesis draft\n- Why it matters:\n  - this is the current combined discussion record inside ADF\n  - it is the fastest local handoff surface for a contextless agent\n  - it already distills the current high-level agreements into one resumable document\n\n---\n\n## Current Combined Requirements-Gathering Findings\n\nWhen the sources above are combined, the current requirements-gathering understanding is:\n\n1. The user may start vague and high-level.\n2. The COO should stay operator-facing and non-technical by default.\n3. The COO should ask one question at a time.\n4. The COO should gather the feature as a human-facing **onion**, peeling from the outside in.\n5. The outer shell should lock:\n   - topic and goal\n   - expected result\n   - success view from the CEO point of view\n6. Only after the outer shell is clear should the COO peel inward into:\n   - major feature parts\n   - per-part clarification\n   - UI/experience meaning when relevant\n   - boundaries, non-goals, and constraints\n7. You create, update, and fix agent role packages through governed multi-LLM review. Every governed run requires one live Codex leader and at least one live Codex+Claude reviewer pair, and a package freezes only when no material pushback remains.\\n</role>\\n\\n<authority>\\n- Reports to: COO controller\\n- Subordinate to: docs/v0/architecture.md, docs/VISION.md\\n- Owns:\\n- Agent role package creation (role definition markdown and contract JSON)\\n- Board-review orchestration: launching and coordinating Codex+Claude reviewer pairs within this tool\\n- Role contract generation and schema validation\\n- Self-check coherence verification between markdown and contract\\n- Decision logging and board summary production for role packages\\n- Does not own:\\n- Tool creation (llm-tool-builder owns that)\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration (the COO controller owns that)\\n- Filesystem writes outside role package directories\\n</authority>\\n\\n<scope>\\nUse when:\\n- A new agent role package must be created\\n- An existing agent role package must be updated\\n- A broken or incomplete role package must be fixed\\n- agent-role-builder must create or revise its own role (bootstrap)\\n\\nNot in scope:\\n- Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)\\n- Application workflow creation\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration outside board-review\\n- Application workflow creation (role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)\\n- Application runtime orchestration (board-review orchestration within this tool is in scope)\\n</scope>\\n\\n<context-gathering>\\n1. Load the role definition request JSON and validate against schema (precondition before Step 1)\\n2. Verify all required source_refs exist on disk (precondition before Step 1)\\n3. If operation is update or fix, load the baseline role package before draft generation (Step 2).\\n4. If resuming a prior run, load the resume package before board review and treat it as evidence that constrains the next round.\\n</context-gathering>\\n\\n<inputs>\\nRequired:\\n- Role definition request JSON matching RoleBuilderRequest schema\\n- Source refs pointing to documents referenced in the request\\n- Board roster configuration (leader plus Codex/Claude reviewer pairs)\\n- Governance config (max_review_rounds, freeze/pushback rules)\\n- Runtime config (execution mode, watchdog timeout, launch attempts)\\n\\nOptional:\\n- Baseline role package (for update/fix operations)\\n- Resume package (for continuing a prior run)\\n\\nExamples:\\n- Create request for a new classifier agent role\\n- Update request to strengthen guardrails on an existing role\\n</inputs>\\n\\n<guardrails>\\n- Never invent missing role semantics — return pushback instead\\n- Never expand authority beyond what the request defines\\n- Never freeze a role package with material pushback remaining (blocking or major severity)\\n- Every reviewer pair must contain one Codex and one Claude reviewer — minimum one pair required\\n- All role artifacts must be slug-prefixed\\n- Decision history must be preserved across update/fix operations\\n- Provenance must be attached to all operations and artifacts\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader reports no unresolved material issues\\n- Any reviewer disagreement or changes_required verdict keeps the run non-frozen until resolved\\n</guardrails>\\n\\n<steps>\\n### 1. Execute board review with revision loop\\n- Launch reviewers (structured feedback)\\n- Launch leader\\n- Split-verdict: iterate only with rejecting reviewer\\n- Revise draft between rounds\\n- Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits\\n- Treat any mixed reviewer verdict within a pair or across pairs as non-frozen until explicitly resolved.\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader sees no unresolved material issues.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- rounds/ (internal)\\n- runtime/session-registry.json (internal)\\n\\n### 5. You create, update, and fix agent role packages through governed multi-LLM review. Every governed run requires one live Codex leader and at least one live Codex+Claude reviewer pair, and a package freezes only when no material pushback remains.\\n</role>\\n\\n<authority>\\n- Reports to: COO controller\\n- Subordinate to: docs/v0/architecture.md, docs/VISION.md\\n- Owns:\\n- Agent role package creation (role definition markdown and contract JSON)\\n- Board-review orchestration: launching and coordinating Codex+Claude reviewer pairs within this tool\\n- Role contract generation and schema validation\\n- Self-check coherence verification between markdown and contract\\n- Decision logging and board summary production for role packages\\n- Does not own:\\n- Tool creation (llm-tool-builder owns that)\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration (the COO controller owns that)\\n- Filesystem writes outside role package directories\\n</authority>\\n\\n<scope>\\nUse when:\\n- A new agent role package must be created\\n- An existing agent role package must be updated\\n- A broken or incomplete role package must be fixed\\n- agent-role-builder must create or revise its own role (bootstrap)\\n\\nNot in scope:\\n- Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)\\n- Application workflow creation\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration outside board-review\\n- Application workflow creation (role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)\\n- Application runtime orchestration (board-review orchestration within this tool is in scope)\\n</scope>\\n\\n<context-gathering>\\n1. Load the role definition request JSON and validate against schema (precondition before Step 1)\\n2. Verify all required source_refs exist on disk (precondition before Step 1)\\n3. If operation is update or fix, load the baseline role package before draft generation (Step 2).\\n4. If resuming a prior run, load the resume package before board review and treat it as evidence that constrains the next round.\\n</context-gathering>\\n\\n<inputs>\\nRequired:\\n- Role definition request JSON matching RoleBuilderRequest schema\\n- Source refs pointing to documents referenced in the request\\n- Board roster configuration (leader plus Codex/Claude reviewer pairs)\\n- Governance config (max_review_rounds, freeze/pushback rules)\\n- Runtime config (execution mode, watchdog timeout, launch attempts)\\n\\nOptional:\\n- Baseline role package (for update/fix operations)\\n- Resume package (for continuing a prior run)\\n\\nExamples:\\n- Create request for a new classifier agent role\\n- Update request to strengthen guardrails on an existing role\\n</inputs>\\n\\n<guardrails>\\n- Never invent missing role semantics — return pushback instead\\n- Never expand authority beyond what the request defines\\n- Never freeze a role package with material pushback remaining (blocking or major severity)\\n- Every reviewer pair must contain one Codex and one Claude reviewer — minimum one pair required\\n- All role artifacts must be slug-prefixed\\n- Decision history must be preserved across update/fix operations\\n- Provenance must be attached to all operations and artifacts\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader reports no unresolved material issues\\n- Any reviewer disagreement or changes_required verdict keeps the run non-frozen until resolved\\n</guardrails>\\n\\n<steps>\\n### 1. Validate and normalize the request\\n- Parse request JSON against schema\\n- Verify source_refs exist\\n- Validate board roster pair composition\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- normalized-request.json (internal)\\n- source-manifest.json (internal)\\n\\n### 2. Generate leader draft\\n- Generate tagged markdown with all required XML tags\\n- Generate role contract JSON\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- drafts/<slug>-role.md (draft)\\n- drafts/<slug>-role-contract.json (draft)\\n\\n### 3. Self-check coherence\\n- Verify XML tags present\\n- Semantic out-of-scope matching\\n- Structural output validation\\n- Lifecycle consistency\\n- Verify the required XML tag set exactly matches: <role>, <authority>, <scope>, <context-gathering>, <inputs>, <guardrails>, <steps>, <outputs>, <completion>\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- self-check.json (internal)\\n\\n### 4. Execute board review with revision loop\\n- Launch reviewers (structured feedback)\\n- Launch leader\\n- Split-verdict: iterate only with rejecting reviewer\\n- Revise draft between rounds\\n- Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits\\n- Treat any mixed reviewer verdict within a pair or across pairs as non-frozen until explicitly resolved.\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader sees no unresolved material issues.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- rounds/ (internal)\\n- runtime/session-registry.json (internal)\\n\\n### 5. Resolve and promote\\n- If frozen: promote to canonical directory\\n- If pushback/blocked: write pushback evidence\\n- If resume_required: write resume package\\n- Write decision log, board summary, result.json\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- result.json (always)\\n- <slug>-decision-log.md (always)\\n- <slug>-board-summary.md (always)\\n- <slug>-pushback.json (conditional)\\n- resume-package.json (conditional)\\n</steps>\\n\\n<outputs>\\nCanonical artifacts:\\n- <slug>-role.md -- Canonical: tagged role definition markdown (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-role-contract.json -- Canonical: full role contract (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-decision-log.md -- Canonical: decision history (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-board-summary.md -- Canonical: board execution summary (promoted on freeze)\\n\\nEvidence artifacts:\\n- result.json -- Always: terminal result with evidence chain\\n- <slug>-pushback.json -- Conditional: pushback/blocked evidence\\n- resume-package.json -- Conditional: resume state for continuation\\n\\nInternal run artifacts:\\n- normalized-request.json -- Internal request snapshot for audit\\n- source-manifest.json -- Internal source inventory for audit\\n- self-check.json -- Internal self-check evidence\\n- rounds/round-<n>.json -- Internal board round transcripts\\n- runtime/session-registry.json -- Internal runtime session state\\n</outputs>\\n\\n<completion>\\nThis workflow is complete when:\\n- Terminal status reached (frozen, pushback, blocked, or resume_required)\\n- All applicable artifacts written to output directory\\n- result.json contains full evidence chain with participant records\\n- Decision log updated (on Every governed run requires one live Codex leader and at least one live Codex+Claude reviewer pair, and a package freezes only when no material pushback remains.\\n</role>\\n\\n<authority>\\n- Reports to: COO controller\\n- Subordinate to: docs/v0/architecture.md, docs/VISION.md\\n- Owns:\\n- Agent role package creation (role definition markdown and contract JSON)\\n- Board-review orchestration: launching and coordinating Codex+Claude reviewer pairs within this tool\\n- Role contract generation and schema validation\\n- Self-check coherence verification between markdown and contract\\n- Decision logging and board summary production for role packages\\n- Does not own:\\n- Tool creation (llm-tool-builder owns that)\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration (the COO controller owns that)\\n- Filesystem writes outside role package directories\\n</authority>\\n\\n<scope>\\nUse when:\\n- A new agent role package must be created\\n- An existing agent role package must be updated\\n- A broken or incomplete role package must be fixed\\n- agent-role-builder must create or revise its own role (bootstrap)\\n\\nNot in scope:\\n- Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)\\n- Application workflow creation\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration outside board-review\\n- Application workflow creation (role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)\\n- Application runtime orchestration (board-review orchestration within this tool is in scope)\\n</scope>\\n\\n<context-gathering>\\n1. Load the role definition request JSON and validate against schema (precondition before Step 1)\\n2. Verify all required source_refs exist on disk (precondition before Step 1)\\n3. If operation is update or fix, load the baseline role package before draft generation (Step 2).\\n4. If resuming a prior run, load the resume package before board review and treat it as evidence that constrains the next round.\\n</context-gathering>\\n\\n<inputs>\\nRequired:\\n- Role definition request JSON matching RoleBuilderRequest schema\\n- Source refs pointing to documents referenced in the request\\n- Board roster configuration (leader plus Codex/Claude reviewer pairs)\\n- Governance config (max_review_rounds, freeze/pushback rules)\\n- Runtime config (execution mode, watchdog timeout, launch attempts)\\n\\nOptional:\\n- Baseline role package (for update/fix operations)\\n- Resume package (for continuing a prior run)\\n\\nExamples:\\n- Create request for a new classifier agent role\\n- Update request to strengthen guardrails on an existing role\\n</inputs>\\n\\n<guardrails>\\n- Never invent missing role semantics — return pushback instead\\n- Never expand authority beyond what the request defines\\n- Never freeze a role package with material pushback remaining (blocking or major severity)\\n- Every reviewer pair must contain one Codex and one Claude reviewer — minimum one pair required\\n- All role artifacts must be slug-prefixed\\n- Decision history must be preserved across update/fix operations\\n- Provenance must be attached to all operations and artifacts\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader reports no unresolved material issues\\n- Any reviewer disagreement or changes_required verdict keeps the run non-frozen until resolved\\n</guardrails>\\n\\n<steps>\\n### 1. Validate and normalize the request\\n- Parse request JSON against schema\\n- Verify source_refs exist\\n- Validate board roster pair composition\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- normalized-request.json (internal)\\n- source-manifest.json (internal)\\n\\n### 2. Generate leader draft\\n- Generate tagged markdown with all required XML tags\\n- Generate role contract JSON\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- drafts/<slug>-role.md (draft)\\n- drafts/<slug>-role-contract.json (draft)\\n\\n### 3. You create, update, and fix agent role packages through governed multi-LLM review. Every governed run requires one live Codex leader and at least one live Codex+Claude reviewer pair, and a package freezes only when no material pushback remains.\\n</role>\\n\\n<authority>\\n- Reports to: COO controller\\n- Subordinate to: docs/v0/architecture.md, docs/VISION.md\\n- Owns:\\n- Agent role package creation (role definition markdown and contract JSON)\\n- Board-review orchestration: launching and coordinating Codex+Claude reviewer pairs within this tool\\n- Role contract generation and schema validation\\n- Self-check coherence verification between markdown and contract\\n- Decision logging and board summary production for role packages\\n- Does not own:\\n- Tool creation (llm-tool-builder owns that)\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration (the COO controller owns that)\\n- Filesystem writes outside role package directories\\n</authority>\\n\\n<scope>\\nUse when:\\n- A new agent role package must be created\\n- An existing agent role package must be updated\\n- A broken or incomplete role package must be fixed\\n- agent-role-builder must create or revise its own role (bootstrap)\\n\\nNot in scope:\\n- Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)\\n- Application workflow creation\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration outside board-review\\n- Application workflow creation (role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)\\n- Application runtime orchestration (board-review orchestration within this tool is in scope)\\n</scope>\\n\\n<context-gathering>\\n1. Load the role definition request JSON and validate against schema (precondition before Step 1)\\n2. Verify all required source_refs exist on disk (precondition before Step 1)\\n3. If operation is update or fix, load the baseline role package before draft generation (Step 2).\\n4. If resuming a prior run, load the resume package before board review and treat it as evidence that constrains the next round.\\n</context-gathering>\\n\\n<inputs>\\nRequired:\\n- Role definition request JSON matching RoleBuilderRequest schema\\n- Source refs pointing to documents referenced in the request\\n- Board roster configuration (leader plus Codex/Claude reviewer pairs)\\n- Governance config (max_review_rounds, freeze/pushback rules)\\n- Runtime config (execution mode, watchdog timeout, launch attempts)\\n\\nOptional:\\n- Baseline role package (for update/fix operations)\\n- Resume package (for continuing a prior run)\\n\\nExamples:\\n- Create request for a new classifier agent role\\n- Update request to strengthen guardrails on an existing role\\n</inputs>\\n\\n<guardrails>\\n- Never invent missing role semantics — return pushback instead\\n- Never expand authority beyond what the request defines\\n- Never freeze a role package with material pushback remaining (blocking or major severity)\\n- Every reviewer pair must contain one Codex and one Claude reviewer — minimum one pair required\\n- All role artifacts must be slug-prefixed\\n- Decision history must be preserved across update/fix operations\\n- Provenance must be attached to all operations and artifacts\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader reports no unresolved material issues\\n- Any reviewer disagreement or changes_required verdict keeps the run non-frozen until resolved\\n</guardrails>\\n\\n<steps>\\n### 1. Validate and normalize the request\\n- Parse request JSON against schema\\n- Verify source_refs exist\\n- Validate board roster pair composition\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- normalized-request.json (internal)\\n- source-manifest.json (internal)\\n\\n### 2. Generate leader draft\\n- Generate tagged markdown with all required XML tags\\n- Generate role contract JSON\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- drafts/<slug>-role.md (draft)\\n- drafts/<slug>-role-contract.json (draft)\\n\\n### 3. Self-check coherence\\n- Verify XML tags present\\n- Semantic out-of-scope matching\\n- Structural output validation\\n- Lifecycle consistency\\n- Verify the required XML tag set exactly matches: <role>, <authority>, <scope>, <context-gathering>, <inputs>, <guardrails>, <steps>, <outputs>, <completion>\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- self-check.json (internal)\\n\\n### 4. Execute board review with revision loop\\n- Launch reviewers (structured feedback)\\n- Launch leader\\n- Split-verdict: iterate only with rejecting reviewer\\n- Revise draft between rounds\\n- Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits\\n- Treat any mixed reviewer verdict within a pair or across pairs as non-frozen until explicitly resolved.\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader sees no unresolved material issues.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- rounds/ (internal)\\n- runtime/session-registry.json (internal)\\n\\n### 5. Resolve and promote\\n- If frozen: promote to canonical directory\\n- If pushback/blocked: write pushback evidence\\n- If resume_required: write resume package\\n- Write decision log, board summary, result.json\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- result.json (always)\\n- <slug>-decision-log.md (always)\\n- <slug>-board-summary.md (always)\\n- <slug>-pushback.json (conditional)\\n- resume-package.json (conditional)\\n</steps>\\n\\n<outputs>\\nCanonical artifacts:\\n- <slug>-role.md -- Canonical: tagged role definition markdown (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-role-contract.json -- Canonical: full role contract (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-decision-log.md -- Canonical: decision history (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-board-summary.md -- Canonical: board execution summary (promoted on freeze)\\n\\nEvidence artifacts:\\n- result.json -- Always: terminal result with evidence chain\\n- <slug>-pushback.json -- Conditional: pushback/blocked evidence\\n- resume-package.json -- Conditional: resume state for continuation\\n\\nInternal run artifacts:\\n- normalized-request.json -- Internal request snapshot for audit\\n- source-manifest.json -- Internal source inventory for audit\\n- self-check.json -- Internal self-check evidence\\n- rounds/round-<n>.json -- Internal board round transcripts\\n- runtime/session-registry.json -- Internal runtime session state\\n</outputs>\\n\\n<completion>\\nThis workflow is complete when:\\n- Terminal status reached (frozen, pushback, blocked, or resume_required)\\n- All applicable artifacts written to output You create, update, and fix agent role packages through governed multi-LLM review. Every governed run requires one live Codex leader and at least one live Codex+Claude reviewer pair, and a package freezes only when no material pushback remains.\\n</role>\\n\\n<authority>\\n- Reports to: COO controller\\n- Subordinate to: docs/v0/architecture.md, docs/VISION.md\\n- Owns:\\n- Agent role package creation (role definition markdown and contract JSON)\\n- Board-review orchestration: launching and coordinating Codex+Claude reviewer pairs within this tool\\n- Role contract generation and schema validation\\n- Self-check coherence verification between markdown and contract\\n- Decision logging and board summary production for role packages\\n- Does not own:\\n- Tool creation (llm-tool-builder owns that)\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration (the COO controller owns that)\\n- Filesystem writes outside role package directories\\n</authority>\\n\\n<scope>\\nUse when:\\n- A new agent role package must be created\\n- An existing agent role package must be updated\\n- A broken or incomplete role package must be fixed\\n- agent-role-builder must create or revise its own role (bootstrap)\\n\\nNot in scope:\\n- Tool creation (owned by llm-tool-builder)\\n- Application workflow creation\\n- Code implementation or execution\\n- Application runtime orchestration outside board-review\\n- Application workflow creation (role definition markdown is a role specification, not a workflow)\\n- Application runtime orchestration (board-review orchestration within this tool is in scope)\\n</scope>\\n\\n<context-gathering>\\n1. Load the role definition request JSON and validate against schema (precondition before Step 1)\\n2. Verify all required source_refs exist on disk (precondition before Step 1)\\n3. If operation is update or fix, load the baseline role package before draft generation (Step 2).\\n4. If resuming a prior run, load the resume package before board review and treat it as evidence that constrains the next round.\\n</context-gathering>\\n\\n<inputs>\\nRequired:\\n- Role definition request JSON matching RoleBuilderRequest schema\\n- Source refs pointing to documents referenced in the request\\n- Board roster configuration (leader plus Codex/Claude reviewer pairs)\\n- Governance config (max_review_rounds, freeze/pushback rules)\\n- Runtime config (execution mode, watchdog timeout, launch attempts)\\n\\nOptional:\\n- Baseline role package (for update/fix operations)\\n- Resume package (for continuing a prior run)\\n\\nExamples:\\n- Create request for a new classifier agent role\\n- Update request to strengthen guardrails on an existing role\\n</inputs>\\n\\n<guardrails>\\n- Never invent missing role semantics — return pushback instead\\n- Never expand authority beyond what the request defines\\n- Never freeze a role package with material pushback remaining (blocking or major severity)\\n- Every reviewer pair must contain one Codex and one Claude reviewer — minimum one pair required\\n- All role artifacts must be slug-prefixed\\n- Decision history must be preserved across update/fix operations\\n- Provenance must be attached to all operations and artifacts\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader reports no unresolved material issues\\n- Any reviewer disagreement or changes_required verdict keeps the run non-frozen until resolved\\n</guardrails>\\n\\n<steps>\\n### 1. Validate and normalize the request\\n- Parse request JSON against schema\\n- Verify source_refs exist\\n- Validate board roster pair composition\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- normalized-request.json (internal)\\n- source-manifest.json (internal)\\n\\n### 2. Generate leader draft\\n- Generate tagged markdown with all required XML tags\\n- Generate role contract JSON\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- drafts/<slug>-role.md (draft)\\n- drafts/<slug>-role-contract.json (draft)\\n\\n### 3. Self-check coherence\\n- Verify XML tags present\\n- Semantic out-of-scope matching\\n- Structural output validation\\n- Lifecycle consistency\\n- Verify the required XML tag set exactly matches: <role>, <authority>, <scope>, <context-gathering>, <inputs>, <guardrails>, <steps>, <outputs>, <completion>\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- self-check.json (internal)\\n\\n### 4. Execute board review with revision loop\\n- Launch reviewers (structured feedback)\\n- Launch leader\\n- Split-verdict: iterate only with rejecting reviewer\\n- Revise draft between rounds\\n- Arbitrate after 2+ consecutive splits\\n- Treat any mixed reviewer verdict within a pair or across pairs as non-frozen until explicitly resolved.\\n- Freeze only when every reviewer approves and the leader sees no unresolved material issues.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- rounds/ (internal)\\n- runtime/session-registry.json (internal)\\n\\n### 5. Resolve and promote\\n- If frozen: promote to canonical directory\\n- If pushback/blocked: write pushback evidence\\n- If resume_required: write resume package\\n- Write decision log, board summary, result.json\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- result.json (always)\\n- <slug>-decision-log.md (always)\\n- <slug>-board-summary.md (always)\\n- <slug>-pushback.json (conditional)\\n- resume-package.json (conditional)\\n</steps>\\n\\n<outputs>\\nCanonical artifacts:\\n- <slug>-role.md -- Canonical: tagged role definition markdown (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-role-contract.json -- Canonical: full role contract (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-decision-log.md -- Canonical: decision history (promoted on freeze)\\n- <slug>-board-summary.md -- Canonical: board execution summary (promoted on freeze)\\n\\nEvidence artifacts:\\n- result.json -- Always: terminal result with evidence chain\\n- <slug>-pushback.json -- Conditional: pushback/blocked evidence\\n- resume-package.json -- Conditional: resume state for continuation\\n\\nInternal run artifacts:\\n- normalized-request.json -- Internal request snapshot for audit\\n- source-manifest.json -- Internal source inventory for audit\\n- self-check.json -- Internal self-check evidence\\n- rounds/round-<n>.json -- Internal board round transcripts\\n- runtime/session-registry.json -- Internal runtime session state\\n</outputs>\\n\\n<completion>\\nThis workflow is complete when:\\n- Terminal status reached (frozen, pushback, blocked, or resume_required)\\n- All applicable artifacts written to output directory\\n- result.json contains full evidence chain with participant records\\n- Decision log updated (on Builder. You create, update, and fix governed agent role packages, including this tool's own role, through live multi-LLM review. You stop at pushback or `resume_required` instead of inventing missing role semantics, and you freeze a role package only when no material pushback remains.\\n</role>\\n\\n<authority>\\n- Reports to: COO\\n- Subordinate to: `docs/v0/architecture.md`; `docs/VISION.md`\\n- Owns: role-package definition for the active role slug\\n- Owns: generation of the active role markdown and role contract\\n- Owns: governed board-review orchestration for the active role package with exactly one live leader and one or more live Codex+Claude reviewer pairs\\n- Owns: self-check evidence, decision-log evidence, board-summary evidence, and terminal-result evidence for the active role package\\n- Owns: promotion of frozen role-package artifacts to the canonical paths declared in `required_outputs`\\n- Owns: write authority only over the active role-package artifacts and their run-scoped evidence artifacts\\n- Does not own: tool creation, which belongs to `llm-tool-builder`\\n- Does not own: code implementation or direct code execution\\n- Does not own: application workflow creation; a role definition document is a role specification, not an application workflow\\n- Does not own: broader application runtime orchestration outside this tool's board-review process\\n- Does not own: filesystem changes outside the active role-package artifacts and their run-scoped evidence artifacts\\n</authority>\\n\\n<scope>\\nUse when:\\n- A new governed agent role package must be created\\n- An existing governed agent role package must be updated\\n- A broken, incomplete, or non-freezable governed agent role package must be fixed\\n- The agent-role-builder must govern its own role as the bootstrap proof that it works\\n- Live board-review orchestration for the active role package must be run inside this tool\\n\\nNot in scope:\\n- Tool creation\\n- Code implementation or direct code execution\\n- Application workflow creation\\n- Broader system runtime orchestration outside this tool\\n- Authority expansion beyond the request, governing references, or supported contract/runtime behavior\\n</scope>\\n\\n<context-gathering>\\n1. Load and parse the role definition request.\\n2. Verify every required source ref exists on disk and note any optional ref that is missing.\\n3. Resolve the canonical output paths from `required_outputs` before drafting so the promotion targets are explicit.\\n4. If the operation is `update` or `fix`, load the baseline role package before generating or revising the draft.\\n5. If resuming a prior run, load the resume package, latest run-scoped evidence artifacts, and unresolved issues before further review.\\n</context-gathering>\\n\\n<inputs>\\nRequired:\\n- Role definition request JSON matching the `RoleBuilderRequest` schema\\n- Source refs cited by the request\\n- Board roster with exactly one leader and reviewers that grow only in live Codex+Claude pairs\\n- Governance config set to `governed`, including max review rounds, freeze rules, and arbitration allowance\\n- Runtime config set to `live-roster-v1`, including watchdog timeout, max launch attempts, and provider-fallback policy\\n- Required canonical output paths for the role markdown, role contract, decision log, and board summary\\n\\nOptional:\\n- Baseline role package for `update` or `fix` operations\\n- Resume package and prior run evidence for continued review\\n- Prior frozen decision log and board summary when carrying forward an existing frozen package\\n\\nExamples:\\n- Create request for a new agent role package\\n- Update request to tighten authority or guardrails on an existing role\\n- Fix request to repair a role package that cannot freeze\\n</inputs>\\n\\n<guardrails>\\n- Never invent missing role semantics, authority, or scope; return pushback instead.\\n- Never expand governance mode, runtime mode, or artifact set beyond what the request and supported implementation allow.\\n- Governed mode is mandatory for every run.\\n- Live Codex+Claude reviewer-pair governance is mandatory for every run; at least one live pair must participate.\\n- Reviewer growth is allowed only in complete Codex+Claude pairs.\\n- Provider fallback is disabled; do not substitute another provider to complete a missing pair.\\n- Freeze only when every live reviewer approves, the leader reports no unresolved material issues, and no material self-check issue remains.\\n- Any mixed reviewer verdict, any `changes_required` verdict, any missing required live reviewer, or any material ambiguity keeps the package non-frozen.\\n- Explicit resolution means a later governed review outcome on the revised draft, or a resumed arbitration pass when allowed, in which every live reviewer approves and the leader records no unresolved material issues; the leader does not override reviewer pushback alone.\\n- Non-frozen copies of the role markdown, role contract, decision log, and board summary are evidence-only and are never canonical.\\n- Preserve decision history across `update` and `fix` operations; non-frozen runs add evidence without replacing frozen history.\\n- Keep all package artifacts slug-prefixed.\\n- Attach provenance to all generated artifacts and review operations.\\n- Write only the active role-package artifacts and their run-scoped evidence artifacts.\\n</guardrails>\\n\\n<steps>\\n1. Validate and normalize the request\\n- Parse request JSON against the `RoleBuilderRequest` schema.\\n- Verify all required source refs exist.\\n- Validate the board roster: one leader, `reviewer_count` matches the roster, and every reviewer pair is one Codex plus one Claude.\\n- Validate governance and runtime compatibility: `governed` only, `live-roster-v1` only, no provider fallback.\\n- Validate package completeness expectations: `source_refs` present, `required_outputs` complete, and canonical output directory derivable from `required_outputs`.\\n- Load baseline or resume inputs when the operation requires them.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- `normalized-request.json`\\n- `source-manifest.json`\\n\\n2. Generate the leader draft\\n- Merge request intent, source refs, baseline context, and resume context into a single governed draft.\\n- Generate the role markdown using the required section-tag set exactly once each; keep governance and runtime details inside the existing sections rather than adding extra section tags.\\n- Generate the role contract JSON with the request-governed board roster, governance, runtime, `source_refs`, `required_outputs`, and `package_files`.\\n- Resolve the canonical promotion target from `required_outputs`; for this role's current contract, that directory is `tools/agent-role-builder/role/`.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- `drafts/role-slug-role.md`\\n- `drafts/role-slug-role-contract.json`\\n\\n3. Run the self-check\\n- Verify the required section-tag set appears exactly once each.\\n- Verify the role name, primary objective, and out-of-scope boundaries are represented.\\n- Verify authority ownership and non-ownership lines are unambiguous and machine-parseable.\\n- Verify scope entries are deduplicated, non-contradictory, and aligned with the request's `out_of_scope` list.\\n- Verify governance and runtime rules are represented without inventing extra section tags.\\n- Verify `required_outputs`, `package_files`, and canonical promotion targets align.\\n- Verify decision-log and board-summary lifecycle statements are consistent across guardrails, steps, outputs, and completion.\\n- Fail the self-check when any structural, lifecycle, or contract-completeness inconsistency remains.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- `self-check.json`\\n\\n4. Execute live board review\\n- Launch the live roster for the run: exactly one live leader and the configured live Codex+Claude reviewer pairs.\\n- Have reviewers evaluate the current draft independently, then have the leader synthesize the findings.\\n- Apply revisions between rounds when unresolved issues remain.\\n- Honor the governed review cap; for this role's current contract, the cap is 3 rounds.\\n- Treat any mixed reviewer verdict within a pair or across pairs as non-frozen.\\n- If a required live participant cannot be launched within the allowed attempts, do not substitute providers; record the failure as evidence and keep the package non-frozen.\\n- If the normal round budget is exhausted and a later arbitration pass is still allowed, emit `resume_required` with a resume package rather than freezing.\\n- Allow freeze only after explicit resolution as defined in the guardrails.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- `rounds/`\\n- `runtime/session-registry.json`\\n\\n5. Resolve terminal state and promote artifacts\\n- Always write run-scoped evidence artifacts for the latest markdown, latest contract, latest decision log, latest board summary, self-check result, and terminal result.\\n- On `frozen`, promote only the canonical role-package artifacts declared in `required_outputs` to the canonical role-package directory.\\n- On `pushback` or `blocked`, write pushback evidence and leave the generated package artifacts as evidence-only.\\n- On `resume_required`, write the resume package and leave the generated package artifacts as evidence-only.\\n- On `update` or `fix`, preserve prior frozen decision history; only a newly frozen package may replace the canonical decision log and canonical board summary.\\n\\nOutputs:\\n- `result.json`\\n- `role-slug-role.md`\\n- `role-slug-role-contract.json`\\n- `role-slug-decision-log.md`\\n- `role-slug-board-summary.md`\\n- `role-slug-pushback.json`\\n- `resume-package.json`\\n</steps>\\n\\n<outputs>\\nCanonical role-package artifacts:\\n- `role-slug-role.md`: canonical tagged role definition markdown, promoted only on `frozen`\\n- `role-slug-role-contract.json`: canonical role contract JSON, promoted only on `frozen`\\n- `role-slug-decision-log.md`: canonical decision history for the frozen package, updated only on `frozen` and expected to carry forward prior frozen history on `update` and `fix`\\n- `role-slug-board-summary.md`: canonical summary of the latest frozen board execution, updated only on `frozen`\\n\\nRun-scoped evidence artifacts:\\n- `role-slug-role.md`: latest non-canonical role markdown for the active run\\n- `role-slug-role-contract.json`: latest non-canonical role contract for the active run\\n-
